business_summary: This database supports a multi-module operations platform used by a manufacturing/industrial company. The core themes are: (1) internal task management (checklists, delegation, housekeeping, maintenance task assignments and their completion/extension), (2) production/quality operational logging for a steel/rolling mill environment (SMS register, QC lab samples, hot coil, recoiler, tundish/ladle/patching checklists), (3) sales pipeline tracking from lead → enquiry → quotation → order follow-ups, and (4) administrative workflows (visitor gate pass, leave requests, store/indent approvals, subscriptions/renewals/payments, document repository, travel/ticket booking, loan/foreclosure/NOC tracking). The schema appears to be an integration of multiple apps (Checklist, Maintenance, Store & Purchase, Sales Module, HRMS, Visitor Gate Pass, Subscription) with shared user access controls and lookup lists (systems, departments, locations, dropdown values).

enquiry_to_order.description: Sales enquiry record that is tracked through follow-ups and potentially converted into an order (enquiry-to-order lifecycle). Captures customer/company details, enquiry metadata, planned vs actual timelines, follow-up scheduling, and order acceptance/payment/transport details.
foreign_keys: Potential link to sales person/user via sales_person_name (string to users.user_name not enforced)
Potential link to sales coordinator via sales_coordinator_name (string to users.user_name not enforced)
Potential link to enquiry receiver via enquiry_receiver_name (string to users.user_name not enforced)
Potential link to client/company dimension via company_name (string)

column_descriptions.id: Surrogate identifier for the enquiry record.
column_descriptions.lead_source: Source channel for the lead/enquiry (Google, Telephonic, Direct Visit).
column_descriptions.item_qty: Requested items and quantities (JSON stored as text).
column_descriptions.planned: Planned target date for next milestone (quote/order/closure depending on process).
column_descriptions.actual: Actual completion date for the planned milestone.
column_descriptions.delay: Delay in days between planned and actual (nullable).
column_descriptions.acceptance_file_upload: Uploaded acceptance/PO file path or blob reference (text).
column_descriptions.if_no_relevant_reason_status: Reason/status when order not received or irrelevant (categorical).
column_descriptions.customer_order_hold_reason_category: Hold reason category when order is on hold.
column_descriptions.holding_date: Date when order was placed on hold.
column_descriptions.sales_coordinator_name: Sales coordinator supporting the sales process.
column_descriptions.planned_days: Planned cycle time in days (target duration).

delegation_done.description: Completion/closure log for delegated tasks, including completion status (done/completed/extend), extension date, reason, and evidence attachment. Appears to be an audit/receipt table for delegation task outcomes.
foreign_keys: task_id likely references delegation.task_id (not enforced)

column_descriptions.task_id: Identifier of the delegated task being updated/completed (from delegation).
column_descriptions.created_at: Timestamp when this completion/extension record was created.
column_descriptions.next_extend_date: New due date when task is extended.
column_descriptions.name: Assignee name or actor name who performed/updated the task.
column_descriptions.task_description: Task text at time of completion (denormalized).
column_descriptions.id: UUID primary key for the done record.

users.description: User master for the platform, including credentials (in plaintext in sample), organizational details, role/status, access control flags for pages/systems/stores, and session token tracking.
foreign_keys: Potential link from leave_request.user_id -> users.id
Implicit organizational links via department/division text fields

column_descriptions.password: Password (appears stored as plain text; should be hashed).
column_descriptions.leave_date: Start time of leave/absence (if used for access control).
column_descriptions.page_access: Comma-separated page routes/features user can access.
column_descriptions.verify_access_dept: Departments the user can verify for.

delegation.description: Task delegation/assignment table for one-time tasks (and potentially recurring in future) with planning dates, reminders, attachment requirements, completion submission dates, and delay calculation.
foreign_keys: Potential relation to delegation_done via task_id
Potential link to users via name (assignee) and given_by (assigner) as text

column_descriptions.task_id: Primary identifier for the delegated task.
column_descriptions.department: Department responsible for executing the task.
column_descriptions.name: Assignee name (person responsible).
column_descriptions.task_start_date: When task is scheduled to start.
column_descriptions.require_attachment: Whether attachment is required for submission.
column_descriptions.delay: Interval difference between planned_date and submission_date.

checklist.description: Recurring/periodic checklist tasks (daily/weekly/monthly etc.) assigned by a supervisor to a doer, with reminder and attachment requirements, planned schedule, submission timestamp, and admin confirmation.
foreign_keys: Potential link to users via name (doer) and given_by (assigner) as text
Potential link to division dimension via division

column_descriptions.task_id: Unique identifier for checklist task instance.
column_descriptions.name: Checklist doer/assignee.
column_descriptions.status: Checklist enabled/active flag (yes/no) or completion indicator (inconsistent casing).
column_descriptions.delay: Interval between planned execution and submission.
column_descriptions.planned_date: Planned date/time for the checklist (stored as text/ISO string).

working_day_calender.description: Calendar dimension table listing working dates with day name (Hindi), week number and month. Used for reporting/filters and schedule computations.

column_descriptions.week_num: Week number of year (or fiscal tracking).

sms_register.description: Steel Melt Shop (SMS) register entries capturing heat/batch sampling events: sequence, ladle, furnace, personnel, temperature, remarks and a unique batch code.
foreign_keys: unique_code/sms_batch_code is likely referenced by hot_coil.sms_short_code, qc_lab_samples.sms_batch_code, pipe_mill.unique_code prefixes, re_coiler/re_coile unique_code (process chain; not enforced)

column_descriptions.id: Primary identifier for the SMS register record.
column_descriptions.laddle_number: Ladle number used in the heat.

indent_approvals.description: Approval events for indent requests, capturing who approved, when, remarks and status.
foreign_keys: indent_id -> indent_requests.id or indent.id (ambiguous due to multiple indent models)
approver_id -> users.employee_id or users.id (string, not enforced)

column_descriptions.indent_id: Reference to indent request needing approval.
column_descriptions.approver_id: Approver identifier (employee code or user id).

ticket_book.description: Travel/ticket booking records including bill metadata, per-ticket and total charges, who requested and who was booked, and bill image upload.
foreign_keys: request_employee_code -> users.employee_id (likely)
booked_employee_code -> users.employee_id (likely)

column_descriptions.status: Workflow status of the ticket booking/bill.
column_descriptions.person_name: Passenger/person for whom ticket is booked.
column_descriptions.booked_name: Person who booked or booking agent name.
column_descriptions.booked_employee_code: Employee code of booked person (if internal).

systems.description: Master list of modules/systems available in the platform and their base URLs.

column_descriptions.link: Base URL for the system frontend.

laddle_checklist.description: Ladle preparation/maintenance checklist for SMS/CCM operations, tracking cleaning, nozzle checks, personnel, plate life, and a unique checklist code.
foreign_keys: Potential process chain via unique_code to other operational records (not enforced)

column_descriptions.id: Row id (nullable; not reliable as PK).
column_descriptions.unique_code: Unique identifier for this checklist submission.

repair_followup.description: Tracking of repair gate pass items sent to vendors and their follow-up stages with planned vs actual dates, delays, and status by stage.

column_descriptions.gate_pass_date: Date gate pass issued for repair.

hot_coil.description: Production/quality capture for hot coil/billet submission events linked to SMS batch short code, including temperatures, supervisors, operator names, and pictures.
foreign_keys: sms_short_code likely references sms_register.unique_code (not enforced)

re_coile.description: Recoiler process log (legacy/alternate table name) capturing recoiling event details including personnel and machine, identified by unique_code.
foreign_keys: unique_code likely links to batch/process chain (e.g., re_coiler.unique_code or pipe_mill.recoiler_short_code prefix)

column_descriptions.unique_code: Unique identifier for the recoiling event/batch.

qc_lab_samples.description: QC lab chemical analysis results for SMS batches/heats, including final chemistry values and report picture.
foreign_keys: sms_batch_code likely references sms_register.unique_code (not enforced)

column_descriptions.sample_timestamp: Timestamp of sample testing or capture.

pipe_mill.description: Pipe mill production log tied to a process unique_code (batch/coil), capturing size, shift, machinery checks (turbine/blower), production timing, supervisors/incharge, and image evidence.
foreign_keys: unique_code likely references sms/hot_coil/re_coiler chain (not enforced)
recoiler_short_code likely references re_coiler.unique_code (prefix) (not enforced)

laddle_return.description: Operational log for ladle return from furnace/CCM including times, temperatures, crane drivers, operators, and reason for return, with unique_code traceability.
foreign_keys: unique_code may link to batch/heat chain (not enforced)

clients.description: Sales client/customer master data with contact information, type segmentation (CRR/NBD/PCRR) and assignment to a salesperson id.
foreign_keys: sales_person_id likely references a salesperson master (not present) or users.id (not enforced)

make_quotation.description: Quotation header data including consigner/consignee details, commercial terms, items (JSON), generated PDF URL, and grand total.
foreign_keys: Potential link to enquiry/lead via reference_name/company_name (string, not enforced)
prepared_by may link to users.user_name (string)

column_descriptions.items: Quoted line items stored as JSON (qty, rate, gst, etc.).

leave_request.description: Employee leave application workflow with date range, reasons, and multi-step approval fields (manager, HR, commercial head).
foreign_keys: user_id -> users.id (likely)

visitors.description: Visitor gate pass log: visitor identity, visit purpose, person to meet, entry/exit times, approval metadata, and closure flag.
foreign_keys: person_to_meet could reference person_to_meet.person_to_meet (string, not enforced)
approved_by could reference users.user_name (string, not enforced)

person_to_meet.description: Directory of employees/contacts that visitors can select as the person to meet.

store_grn.description: Store Goods Receipt Note (GRN) bill tracking for vendor invoices including admin/GM approval flags and bill closure.

column_descriptions.sended_bill: Whether bill was sent/submitted for approval.

plant_visitor.description: Request workflow for planned plant visits (likely for groups/official visits) including requester, date range, number of persons, and approval metadata.
foreign_keys: employee_code / approv_employee_code could link to users.employee_id (not enforced)

indent.description: Store/Purchase indent line-level records captured from operational departments for materials/tools/spares. Includes request metadata, item details, planned vs actual timeline, and approval outcome including GM approval.

column_descriptions.time_delay_1: Delay between planned and actual (stored as text interval).

departments.description: Department master list with HOD and contact number.

indent_requests.description: Indent request header (separate from indent line table) capturing indent number, request date, requester, priority and status.

indent_items.description: Indent request line items for indent_requests; each row is an item name and quantity requested with current available stock.
foreign_keys: indent_id -> indent_requests.id (likely)

column_descriptions.available_stock: Stock on hand at request time.

enquiry_tracker.description: Event-style tracker for enquiry follow-ups and stage changes; multiple rows per enquiry capturing status, next call schedule, and order decision details.
foreign_keys: enquiry_no may link to fms_leads.lead_no or enquiry_to_order.en_enquiry_no (not enforced)
order_no may link to internal order system (not present)

column_descriptions.id: Tracker row id (nullable in schema but present in data).
column_descriptions.timestamp: When this tracker update was recorded.
column_descriptions.calling_days: Call aging / number of calling days.

size_master.description: Product size/thickness master for different item types (round/square/rectangular). Used for standardization in ERP/enquiry capture.

fms_leads.description: Lead management table (likely imported from an external FMS/CRM) capturing lead intake, enquiry conversion info, follow-up plan, and order outcome fields. Many fields are stored as TEXT including dates.
foreign_keys: lead_no links to enquiry_tracker.enquiry_no and leads_tracker.lead_no (not enforced)
salesperson_name/sc_name likely correspond to users.user_name (not enforced)

column_descriptions.actual: Actual date completed for lead stage.
column_descriptions.enquiry_received_status: Whether enquiry was received from lead (yes/no).

leads_tracker.description: Lead follow-up tracker (but sample suggests columns/data are mis-mapped or corrupted). Intended to store lead stage, follow-up status, next call date and calling day counts.
foreign_keys: lead_no likely references fms_leads.lead_no (not enforced)

column_descriptions.lead_no: Lead identifier (expected LD-xxx; sample appears shifted).
column_descriptions.location: Location or items JSON (sample indicates misloaded).

dropdown.description: Lookup/value repository powering UI dropdowns for the sales module (lead sources, enquiry status, payment modes, product categories, contact details templates, etc.). Functions as a semi-master table of allowed values.

enq_erp.description: ERP-style enquiry capture at SKU level (item type/size/thickness/quantity) by customer and sales executive on a given enquiry date.
foreign_keys: size/thickness likely align with size_master (not enforced)
sales_executive could link to users.user_name (not enforced)

client_followups.description: Client calling/follow-up log per client and salesperson; may store actual order amount/date when conversion happens.
foreign_keys: client_name could link to clients.client_name (string, not enforced)

request.description: Generic request table used for travel and possibly recruitment manpower requests (mixed fields: travel cities/dates and experience/education).
foreign_keys: employee_code likely references users.employee_id (not enforced)

re_coiler.description: Recoiler process log tied to SMS batch short code, capturing personnel, machine, and unique_code for traceability (more complete than re_coile).
foreign_keys: hot_coiler_short_code likely references sms_register.unique_code or hot_coil.sms_short_code (not enforced)
unique_code used by pipe_mill.recoiler_short_code (likely)

login.description: Legacy/alternate user login table (appears redundant with users). Stores credentials, role, department and access fields.

maintenance_task_assign.description: Maintenance task assignment and execution tracking for machines/assets with scheduling, reminders, optional sound/temperature checks, attachments, cost capture, and completion status.
foreign_keys: serial_no and machine_name may reference form_responses (asset register) (not enforced)
given_by/doer_name may map to users.user_name (not enforced)

column_descriptions.time_stamp: Timestamp of task scheduling/creation in source form.
column_descriptions.temperature: Whether temperature check is required (stored as text).

assign_task.description: Housekeeping/general task submission log by department/location, with frequency, submission time, optional image/attachment confirmation, and delay metric.
foreign_keys: department likely references locations.location (not enforced)
given_by/name/doer_name2 might map to users.user_name (not enforced)

column_descriptions.task_id: Task identifier (string but same as numeric in sample).
column_descriptions.name: Group/role name (e.g., Housekeeping Staff).

locations.description: Master list of plant/office locations/areas used for tasks/checklists/housekeeping.

form_responses.description: Asset register / machine master captured from a form: serial numbers, purchase/warranty, vendor/manufacturer, maintenance schedule, location, and associated documents.

column_descriptions.serial_no: Serial number string (also used as tag in sample).
column_descriptions.user_allot: Assigned user or allotment (in sample appears inconsistent).

master.description: Lightweight master/task registry (likely legacy) capturing doer, departments, assigner, task type/status and priority.

all_loans.description: Register of all company loans with EMI schedule, start/end dates, and document storage.

approval_history.description: Approval audit trail for subscription approvals, storing approval number, status, approver, and timestamps.
foreign_keys: subscription_no -> subscription.subscription_no (not enforced)

resume_request.description: Candidate/resume tracking for recruitment requests, including interview planned/actual, interviewer status, candidate status and joining status.
foreign_keys: req_id may link to request.request_no (if used for hiring) (not enforced)

collect_noc.description: Loan closure NOC collection tracking: identifies loan by serial_no and records whether NOC has been collected after closure request.
foreign_keys: serial_no likely links to request_forclosure.serial_no or all_loans id/serial mapping (not enforced)

subscription.description: Subscription contract tracking (likely for software/services) including planned vs actual workflow stages, renewal counters, approval status, contract period, document copy, and pricing.
foreign_keys: subscription_no referenced by approval_history, payment_history, subscription_renewals

payment_history.description: Payments recorded against subscriptions, capturing mode, transaction id, start date, and supporting document.
foreign_keys: subscription_no -> subscription.subscription_no (likely)

column_descriptions.insurance_document: Supporting document link (field name suggests insurance but used generically).

request_forclosure.description: Loan foreclosure/closure request records, capturing loan details and who requested closure.

sharedocuments.description: Outbound document sharing log: who shared what document, to which email/number, how it was shared, and metadata for categorization.

subscription_renewals.description: Renewal transactions for subscriptions, including renewal number, status, approver, price and timestamp.
foreign_keys: subscription_no -> subscription.subscription_no (likely)

documents.description: Central document repository for company/personal documents with tagging, renewal reminders, and soft deletion.

payment_fms.description: Payment request/workflow (FMS) with multi-stage planned/actual dates and delays, attachments, and payment type/status.

column_descriptions.id: UUID id for payment workflow row.

subscription_master.description: Master configuration for subscriptions/documents by company, including document_type/category and whether renewal filtering is enabled.

patching_checklist.description: Furnace/crucible patching checklist capturing timing, materials, multiple checks (boolean), responsible person, and unique_code for traceability.

tundish_checklist.description: Tundish preparation checklist for casting operations with multiple checks and handover confirmations, personnel names, remarks, and unique_code.

suggested_semantic_schema:
DATABASE PURPOSE
This database supports multiple operational modules for an industrial manufacturing company:
- Task Management: Delegation (one-time tasks), Checklists (recurring tasks), Housekeeping submissions, Maintenance task assignments, and their completion/extension logs.
- Production & Quality Logs: SMS (Steel Melt Shop) register, QC lab chemistry samples, Hot coil submissions, Recoiler operations, Pipe mill logs, and operational checklists (Ladle, Tundish, Patching, Ladle return).
- Sales CRM: Lead intake, enquiry tracking, quotation generation, enquiry-to-order tracking, and client follow-up calling.
- Admin Workflows: Visitor gate passes, leave requests, store GRN approvals, indents, subscriptions/renewals/payments, documents repository, travel/ticket requests, and loan/NOC processes.

CORE ENTITIES & HOW TO QUERY
1) USERS & ACCESS
- Table: users
  - PK: users.id
  - Use users.user_name and users.employee_id for joining to workflows where only text codes exist.
  - Role: users.role (admin/user). Division: users.division.

2) TASKS (CHECKLIST / DELEGATION / HOUSEKEEPING / MAINTENANCE)
- delegation: one-time delegated tasks
  - PK: delegation.task_id
  - Status: delegation.status (Done/extend), dates: planned_date, submission_date, delay
- delegation_done: completion/extension log
  - PK: delegation_done.id, relates to delegation via task_id
- checklist: recurring checklist tasks
  - PK: checklist.task_id
  - Key analytics: frequency, planned_date, submission_date, delay, admin_done, user_status_checklist, division
- assign_task: housekeeping/general task submissions
  - PK: assign_task.id; business key often assign_task.task_id
  - Key analytics: department (location), frequency, submission_date, delay, status
- maintenance_task_assign: maintenance tasks for assets
  - PK: maintenance_task_assign.id
  - Task number: task_no; asset keys: serial_no, machine_name
  - Key analytics: task_start_date, actual_date, task_status, delay, maintenance_cost
- form_responses: asset register
  - PK: form_responses.id; identify assets by serial_no/tag_no

3) PRODUCTION / QUALITY TRACEABILITY (BATCH CODES)
Traceability often uses a batch/heat short code (e.g., 25B01) and derived unique codes:
- sms_register.unique_code: base heat/batch code
- hot_coil.sms_short_code and hot_coil.unique_code: refers to SMS batch
- re_coiler.unique_code: derived recoiler code (e.g., 25B01A)
- pipe_mill.unique_code: derived pipe mill code (e.g., 25B01A01) and pipe_mill.recoiler_short_code (e.g., 25B01A)
- qc_lab_samples.sms_batch_code: SMS batch code
Operational checklists have their own unique_code fields (ladle_checklist.unique_code, tundish_checklist.unique_code, patching_checklist.unique_code).

4) SALES PIPELINE
- fms_leads: lead intake + conversion fields (many dates stored as TEXT)
  - Business key: lead_no (LD-xxx)
- enquiry_tracker: event tracker for enquiry/lead follow-ups
  - Links via enquiry_no (often lead_no or EN-xx)
- enquiry_to_order: enriched enquiry record with customer details and order acceptance/payment/transport
- make_quotation: quotation header with items JSON and pdf_url
- clients: client master; client_followups: calling log per client

5) STORE & PURCHASE
- indent: line-level requisition/indent entries with approval outcome
- indent_requests (header) + indent_items (lines) + indent_approvals (approval events)
- store_grn: GRN bill tracking and approvals

6) HR / VISITOR
- leave_request: leave workflow (user_id may link to users.id)
- visitors: visitor gate pass log; person_to_meet directory

7) SUBSCRIPTIONS & DOCUMENTS
- subscription: subscription contracts with planned/actual stages
- subscription_renewals: renewal events
- approval_history: approval audit for subscriptions
- payment_history: payments for subscriptions
- documents: central repository with renewal tracking
- sharedocuments: outbound sharing log

JOINING GUIDANCE FOR TEXT-TO-SQL
- Prefer numeric PK joins where present (users.id, subscription_no, etc.).
- Many relationships are not enforced and use names/codes as TEXT; when joining users to workflow tables, match on users.user_name or users.employee_id depending on field content.
- Dates are inconsistently typed across sales tables (TEXT vs DATE/TIMESTAMP). Cast where needed (e.g., to_date(next_call_date,'YYYY-MM-DD')).

COMMON METRICS
- Task compliance: count tasks by status, delay, submission_date vs planned_date, grouped by department/division/doer/given_by.
- Sales funnel: leads by source/status, enquiry follow-up aging (calling_days), conversion to order (is_order_received/order_no), quotation totals (grand_total).
- Production QA: QC chemistry by sms_batch_code, temperatures and checklist completion rates by shift/furnace/ladle.
- Approvals: indent approvals and GRN approvals aging; subscription approvals and renewals with payment history.
